name: Portuguese Sweet Bread (Pao Doce)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup lukewarm scalded milk, then cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water in a large bowl.
2. Stir in the scalded milk, 3/4 cup sugar, salt, 3 eggs, and 3 cups of flour. Beat until smooth.
3. Add enough remaining flour to make the dough easy to handle. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface.
4. Knead until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes.
5. Place in a greased bowl. Turn the greased side up. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, 1 1/2 to 2 hours. (The dough is ready if an indentation remains when touched.)
6. Punch down the dough and divide into halves.
7. Shape each half into round, slightly flat loaves. Place each loaf in a greased round 9x1-1/2-inch layer cake pan.
8. Cover and let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
9. Preheat oven to 350°F.
10. Beat the egg lightly and brush over the loaves. Sprinkle with 1 teaspoon sugar.
11. Bake until the loaves are golden brown, 35 to 45 minutes.
SNAIL LOAVES (CARACOIS):
After dividing the dough into halves, roll each half into a rope about 25x1-1/2 inches. Coil each into a snail shape in a greased round 9-inch layer cake pan. Proceed as directed.
